pyramid_page.title: Project Pyramid
pyramid_page.subtitle: A stealth-adventure set in ancient Egypt, featuring a modular AI architecture and advanced sensory systems.
pyramid_page.banner: AI class structure and Subsystem logic in JetBrains Rider
status_note: Project Pyramid is an ongoing collaborative team project.
My role as AI Systems Programmer is currently concluded/paused after delivering the core enemy architecture and perception framework.
The team is now proceeding with level design, asset creation, and puzzle mechanics.

concept.title: Stealth AI Engineering
desc: In a stealth-focused experience, the AI is the heart of the gameplay loop. I was responsible for designing a system that wasn't just 'reactive', but believable.

From managing enemy memory via the Dummy System to reinforcement coordination, the goal was to create a scalable framework that designers could easily tune via Data Tables without touching the codebase.

tech_work.title: Core Systems & AI
items: • Dummy Entity World Subsystem: Implemented a C++ system that spawns a 'ghost' entity at the player's last known position. AI units investigate the Dummy instead of 'cheating' with omniscience, creating realistic search patterns.

• Multi-State AI (C++/BT): Developed a modular Behavior Tree architecture with 5 primary states (Patrol, Alert, Search, Combat, Dead). It features dynamic perception management that live-updates sensory parameters based on the current state.

• Coordination & Roles: Implemented a synchronized 'Call for Aid' system to prevent overlapping enemy yells. Added role assignment logic (Investigator vs. Support) to ensure enemies surround the player during investigations.
• EQS & Flanking: Leveraged the Environment Query System for dynamic flanking in combat and to claim exclusive search points, preventing multiple AI agents from stacking on the same spot.
• Slide Component: Built a standalone, slope-aware sliding mechanic for the player character using vector projection and VLerp of terrain normals for smooth traversal.

lessons.title: Workflow & Tooling
desc: I focused on system optimization by migrating Tick-based components (like footstep detection) to high-performance Anim Notifies.

To improve team iteration, I created editor tools via Construction Scripts to visualize patrol paths, indices, and wait times directly in the viewport, removing the need to check individual array elements.

highlights.title: Key Technical Contributions
items: ⁕ Dynamic Flanking and Point Claiming via EQS
⁕ Data Table-driven AI for rapid balancing
⁕ Custom Editor Tooling for Spline Patrols
